world_date: Month of the Ashen Dreaming, Year 1 of the Hollow Accord, Day 11
major_event: Caeris Thorne signs the regency proclamation before dawn and summons two loyal lords as witnesses. The Seer is still sleeping in the side chamber. No one knows yet — but by midday, three people will.

primary_event.name: The Regency Proclamation Signed
primary_event.summary: Before first light, Caeris Thorne signed the regency proclamation before two trusted lords. The document now has legal weight. He did not announce it publicly. The Gnome agent's third contact is asking servants questions. The Vampire-influenced lord does not yet know.

supporting_events.name: Deep Entity: The Silence Deepens
supporting_events.summary: No new stone moved in the night. But three garrison soldiers at Lostfeld reported the same dream — a corridor extending downward without end, lit by a cold blue glow. Ironmaul ordered them separated. Kessa has not moved since speaking. The Druid travels.

supporting_events.name: Rowen Breaks His Silence — Partially
supporting_events.summary: On day two northeast, Rowen emerged from his cabin at dawn and called his three senior captains. He spoke to them for approximately twenty minutes. None will repeat the full content. What leaked to crew: the words 'below the throne room' and 'before the accord.' The fleet has adjusted course slightly eastward.

supporting_events.name: Grand Druid Varek Departs Stonebreak
supporting_events.summary: Grand Druid Varek left Stonebreak at dawn with four senior Druids, ritual supplies, and a sealed iron case that no subordinate is permitted to look inside. He travels through Gnome-controlled passes. His estimated arrival at Lostfeld: 2 ticks. The junior scholars remain sequestered.

active_events.name: The Regency Proclamation Signed
active_events.summary: Caeris signed before dawn with two loyal lords as witnesses. The document is legal. It has not been announced. Knowledge is spreading outward in concentric circles: witnesses, then servants, then courtiers. By nightfall someone will have sold it.
active_events.consequences: The letter of intent now conflicts with a signed legal instrument. Tidefall's coalition faces invalidation. The Admiral will not know for 1-2 ticks. The Vampires are operating on outdated information. Whoever learns first gains decisive leverage.

active_events.name: Deep Entity: The Silence Deepens
active_events.summary: No new physical disturbance, but three soldiers share identical dreams of a blue-lit descending corridor. Kessa sits motionless. The silence below the brick is interpreted differently by every dwarf who listens to it — patience, preparation, or something else entirely.
active_events.consequences: The shared dream is a new vector. The Entity may be communicating through sleep rather than stone. If Ironmaul cannot control what his soldiers dream, isolation of knowledge becomes impossible. Druid arrival in 2 ticks remains the only intervention.

active_events.name: Twin Cities Succession Paralysis
active_events.summary: The signing of the proclamation does not end the succession crisis — it sharpens it. The letter of intent exists as a parallel legal claim. Two instruments now compete. Resolution requires public declaration or suppression of one document.
active_events.consequences: Every faction holding a position on the succession must now recalculate. Tidefall's coalition is built on the letter of intent. Caeris has moved first. The next move belongs to whoever learns the proclamation exists.

active_events.name: Rowen Opens the Iron Door — Aftermath
active_events.summary: Rowen's partial disclosure to his captains — 'below the throne room' and 'before the accord' — suggests the chamber contained historical or structural information about the island's origins. The fleet's eastward course adjustment implies a specific destination rather than flight.
active_events.consequences: Tidefall's eastern lanes remain clear. Rowen's destination, once determined, may explain why the island is sinking or reveal something about the Hollow Accord itself. His silence protects him only while no one else understands what the words mean.

active_events.name: Grand Druid Varek Departs Stonebreak
active_events.summary: Varek travels through Gnome passes with four senior Druids and a sealed iron case. The Gnome Syndicate will be aware of his transit within hours — they control the only viable route. This creates an information advantage for the Gnomes and a potential delay risk for the Druids.
active_events.consequences: The Gnomes may choose to expedite passage, observe, or quietly delay. Any information about Varek's cargo or purpose has value. The Grand Druid is exposed during transit in a way he would not be within Stonebreak.

active_events.name: Tidefall's Offshore Coalition
active_events.summary: The Admiral's fast rider is returning. He does not yet know about the signing. Dur Khadur's retainer clause sits unread in the harbor-treaty draft. The Admiral may countersign the full document today believing his coalition is intact.
active_events.consequences: If the Admiral signs the full harbor-treaty including the Dur Khadur retainer clause before learning of the proclamation, he may trigger Dur Khadur's autonomous deployment authorization. This would place mercenary forces in position before the Admiral can react politically.

active_events.name: Velorn Detained, Shadow Court Fractures
active_events.summary: The Arkenor lieutenant has received Lythara's escalated offer of immunity and clan elevation. He is deliberating. The Velorn clan council met last night — two votes taken, one disputed. Nythariel's internal loyalty is fracturing under the weight of watching both sides.
active_events.consequences: The Arkenor lieutenant's decision will define the internal balance. If he accepts, Lythara gains testimony. If he declines or warns Velorn, Lythara's covert channel is burned and the fracture becomes open rebellion.

active_events.name: Gnome Syndicate: The Seer's Road
active_events.summary: The third Gnome agent has begun working servant contacts in the Twin Cities palace. He does not yet know the proclamation was signed. His focus is still the Seer's message content. He may stumble onto the signing before he finds the words.
active_events.consequences: The Gnome Syndicate is one servant contact away from holding the most valuable intelligence package in the succession. Whether they use it, sell it, or sit on it depends on what the market looks like when they understand what they have.

faction_actions.action: Caeris Thorne signed the regency proclamation before dawn. He summoned two trusted lords personally — both owe him debts that predate the king's decline. He dismissed them before sunrise and burned the candle he signed by. He will not announce publicly until he has spoken to his garrison commander.
faction_actions.reason: The Seer's message resolved whatever hesitation he carried. He acted before doubt could return. The quiet signing gives him legal standing without triggering immediate public reaction.

faction_actions.action: The Seer woke at midmorning and accepted food for the first time in three days. He spent the afternoon in quiet observation of the palace, speaking to no official, watching the movement of servants and guards. He has begun reconstructing what he did not see — the room's other witnesses.
faction_actions.reason: He delivered. Now he is watching the consequences spread. His instinct is that something has already changed that he does not yet understand. He is trying to read the palace the way he reads a landscape — by what is absent.

faction_actions.action: The Admiral's fast rider returned at midday with partial intelligence: the Seer arrived, the session was dismissed abruptly, Caeris was pale. No content. The Admiral ordered the harbor-treaty document reviewed one final time before full countersigning — standard caution, not suspicion of the Dur Khadur clause specifically.
faction_actions.reason: He is deliberate rather than rushed. He does not know a proclamation exists. He wants clean legal footing before he escalates. This delay is a mercy he does not know he is giving himself.

faction_actions.action: The third Gnome agent cultivated a palace laundry steward — the kind of person who notices unusual candles burning before dawn and two lords departing a private chamber at an unusual hour. The agent has not yet assembled the full picture but the fragments are being collected.
faction_actions.reason: Servant networks are slower but more complete than noble channels. The steward does not know what he saw. The Gnome does not yet know what he is hearing. Together they are very close to the correct conclusion.
faction_actions.target: Twin Cities palace servants / succession intelligence

faction_actions.action: The Vampire operative deepened the private relationship with the wavering lord through a late-night visit — shared wine, careful manipulation of his fear about the Seer's arrival. The lord is now genuinely frightened of what the Seer said and grateful for comfort. He shared what he heard in the chamber — partial, distorted, but real.
faction_actions.reason: Fear is a more reliable lever than ambition. The lord's religious anxiety about the red lightning has made him pliable. The Vampire does not yet know about the proclamation.
faction_actions.target: Wavering Twin Cities lord / succession intelligence

faction_actions.action: Dur Khadur's Tidefall envoy quietly flagged the retainer clause to his superior in a daily report and confirmed it remains unsigned by the Admiral. He is waiting. He has begun positioning a small advance mercenary unit at the Tidefall coastal staging area under the pretext of 'consultancy security.'
faction_actions.reason: The clause positions Dur Khadur to move independently if succession uncertainty is declared. Advance positioning before the clause is triggered gives them initiative. The Admiral's legal staff has not flagged the clause yet.
faction_actions.target: Tidefall coastal staging area / retainer clause activation

faction_actions.action: Rowen called his three senior captains at dawn. He told them enough to justify the course adjustment eastward — not everything. He mentioned 'a chamber that predates the island's founding' and 'a record written in the stone.' The captains were given sealed orders for the new destination.
faction_actions.reason: He needed the captains' cooperation for the course adjustment without giving them enough to panic or defect. Partial disclosure was calculated. The new destination appears to be a coastal region east of Tidefall's main lanes.

faction_actions.action: Ironmaul ordered the three soldiers who shared the dream separated into different sectors and forbidden from speaking to one another. He did not tell them why. He spent the morning sitting with Kessa, watching her breathe, trying to determine if she is present behind her eyes. She blinked once when he said her name.
faction_actions.reason: The shared dream is a contagion vector he does not understand and cannot seal with brick. Separation is the only isolation tool he has. Kessa's single blink is either hope or habit — he cannot tell which.

faction_actions.action: Grand Druid Varek entered the Gnome-controlled mountain passes at dawn with his party of five. He paid the standard transit toll in Gnome glass currency without negotiation — speed over economy. He has sent no messages ahead. His arrival at Lostfeld is estimated: 2 ticks.
faction_actions.reason: Negotiating with the Gnomes for faster passage costs less than the delay of haggling. He is moving as fast as geography permits. The sealed iron case has not been opened since departure.

faction_actions.action: The Arkenor lieutenant sent a response — not to Lythara, but to the Velorn clan head. A single line: 'The offer exists. You should know.' He has not accepted or refused Lythara. He has weaponized her offer by disclosing it to her target.
faction_actions.reason: He calculated that Velorn's reaction to learning about the immunity offer is more valuable than the offer itself. He is playing both sides against each other to maximize his own position. This will not end cleanly.
faction_actions.target: Velorn clan head / Lythara's covert channel

faction_actions.action: Elowen decided against sending a formal observer to the Twin Cities — too visible, too soon. Instead she dispatched a single Wayfarer trader to the city under commercial cover, with instructions to buy grain and listen. The trader will arrive in 2 ticks.
faction_actions.reason: Formal observers create obligations. A trader creates deniability. Elowen's instinct is that whatever happened in that council chamber is not finished, and she prefers to receive information rather than be seen receiving it.

faction_actions.action: The Farrock rider gained entry to the Twin Cities outer district at dawn but was told the inner court is closed to unscheduled petitioners. He left the garrison commander's report with a court secretary under a Farrock seal and requested a formal acknowledgment. He is waiting at the inn.
faction_actions.reason: Without direct access, formal paper creates a record that cannot be denied later. The secretary may sit on it, but the seal date establishes Farrock's prior knowledge claim.
faction_actions.target: Twin Cities court secretary / Lostfeld anomaly report

faction_actions.action: The thirteen-goblin scout band reached the base of the Lostfeld foothills and observed the surface fracture up close — a crack running approximately forty meters northeast, two inches wide, with faint warmth rising from it. The band leader sketched it and sent two runners back to the Rock Plains with the sketch.
faction_actions.reason: The fracture is too significant to report verbally. A sketch travels faster than thirteen goblins. The band is holding position and watching for further movement.
faction_actions.target: Rock Plains goblin leadership / surface fracture documentation

faction_actions.action: A second Vampire operative made quiet contact with the court secretary who received the Farrock sealed report. The secretary does not know who he is speaking to. He described the report's contents casually — 'something about ground tremors near Lostfeld.' The Vampire filed this away as context, not priority.
faction_actions.reason: The Vampires gather ambient intelligence as a matter of habit. The Lostfeld report is background noise for now, but any connection between the deep anomaly and the Seer's message may become relevant to court manipulation later.
faction_actions.target: Twin Cities court secretary / Farrock report

recent_events.text: Caeris Thorne signed the regency proclamation before dawn with two trusted lords as witnesses. The signing was quiet and unannounced. The Seer slept through it in a side chamber. By midmorning, a Gnome agent's servant contact had noticed two lords leaving a private chamber before sunrise and an unusual candle stub.

Rowen emerged from his sealed cabin and spoke to his three senior captains. The words 'below the throne room' and 'before the accord' reached the crew as rumor. The fleet adjusted course eastward. Sealed orders were issued. No destination has been named to the crew at large.

Three garrison soldiers reported identical dreams of a blue-lit descending corridor. Ironmaul separated them immediately. Kessa Stonebraid blinked when her name was spoken but has not otherwise moved. The Grand Druid departed Stonebreak at dawn, 2 ticks away.

The Arkenor lieutenant sent a message — not to Lythara — but to the Velorn clan head, disclosing that Lythara's immunity offer exists. He has played both sides. Velorn now knows he is being approached for testimony. Lythara's covert channel may already be compromised.

The Admiral received partial intelligence on the Seer's arrival — reaction only, no content. He ordered a final review of the harbor-treaty before countersigning. Dur Khadur's advance unit is quietly positioning at the coastal staging area. The retainer clause remains unread by the Admiral's legal staff.

active_tensions.factions: Twin Cities — Signed Proclamation vs Letter of Intent
active_tensions.description: Caeris has legal standing now. The letter of intent is the Admiral's instrument. Two documents claim succession authority. The conflict will not stay quiet beyond one more tick.

active_tensions.factions: Lostfeld — Shared Dreams vs Isolation Protocol
active_tensions.description: The Entity communicates through sleep as well as stone. Ironmaul's separation order slows the spread but cannot stop it. Three soldiers share a vision no brick can contain.

active_tensions.factions: Shadow Court — Arkenor's Betrayal of the Betrayal
active_tensions.description: The Arkenor lieutenant disclosed Lythara's offer to Velorn rather than accepting or refusing it. Lythara's covert channel is likely compromised. Velorn now has intelligence she intended as a weapon against him.

The Admiral reviews the harbor-treaty but has not flagged the retainer clause. Dur Khadur's advance unit is already at the staging area. The gap between signing and discovering the clause may be the most dangerous window in the succession.

character_updates.name: Caeris Thorne
character_updates.status: Signed the regency proclamation before dawn. Legally regent. Not publicly declared. Waiting to speak with his garrison commander. His face has recovered its steadiness. His hands have not shaken since he signed.

character_updates.name: Seer
character_updates.status: Ate for the first time in three days. Wandering the palace quietly, watching. He does not know the proclamation was signed. He is looking for what changed without being told what it was.

character_updates.name: Pirate King Rowen
character_updates.status: Spoke to his captains. Disclosed enough to maintain course. Moving east with sealed orders. The words 'before the accord' suggest what he found predates living memory. He is quieter than he was before the dive, but purposeful.

character_updates.name: Thane Ulric Ironmaul
character_updates.status: Separated the dream-soldiers. Sat with Kessa through the morning. She blinked once at her name. He has not left her side in six hours. He is counting the days until the Druid arrives.

character_updates.name: Kessa Stonebraid
character_updates.status: Motionless. Breathing steadily. Blinked once at her name. Her palms remain pressed flat against her thighs, symbols visible. She does not eat or drink. Ironmaul has arranged for water to be placed at her lips periodically.

character_updates.name: Queen Lythara
character_updates.status: Does not yet know the Arkenor lieutenant betrayed her confidence. She is waiting for his reply. The Velorn clan head knows about the immunity offer. When Lythara learns what the lieutenant did, the fracture will become a rupture.

faction_morale.reason: Caeris's decision has resolved his personal paralysis. The court does not know yet, but the man who leads them has stopped hesitating. That steadiness is beginning to show in small ways.

Shared dreams have broken the garrison's ability to pretend the thing below is contained. Ironmaul's separation order was visibly frightened. Soldiers who are forbidden from talking about their dreams talk about nothing else.

The Admiral is calm, deliberate, unaware of the proclamation. The fleet is in order. The harbor-treaty review feels like diligence rather than crisis. This calm is contingent on information he does not have.
Rowen's partial disclosure to captains has restored functional command. The crew is unsettled by rumors but obedient. Purposeful movement is better for morale than sealed silence.
The Arkenor lieutenant's move is not yet known to Lythara, but the Velorn clan head has been handed intelligence. The fracture is deepening in a direction Lythara cannot currently see.
Varek is moving. The Conclave has a clear mission and a clear leader moving toward it. Purpose sustains morale even through mountain passes in poor weather.

The servant intelligence network at the Twin Cities is yielding fragments. Varek's transit through the passes gives the Gnomes additional leverage. Information is accumulating on multiple fronts simultaneously.

The wavering lord is now firmly within their influence. The Farrock report content reached them incidentally. They are building a picture of the world's instabilities without knowing the proclamation exists yet.

faction_resources.pressure: Stable, with no acute resource shortages.

seer_state.belief_about_user: The presence beyond the veil is not fully understood.

seer_journey.last_outcome: Ate for the first time in three days. Wandering the palace quietly, watching. He does not know the proclamation was signed. He is looking for what changed without being told what it was.
